ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text of the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was posted */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet received */,
  "retweets" INTEGER /* Number of retweets the tweet received */,
  "place_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the tweet was posted */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "search_terms" TEXT /* Search terms used to collect the tweet */,
  "filter_terms" TEXT /* Filter terms used to filter the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_place_id FOREIGN KEY ("place_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, administrator) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code of the location */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., city, country, region) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword extracted from the tweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet where the keyword was found */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_keywords_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "hashtag" TEXT /* Hashtag extracted from the tweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet where the hashtag was found */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hashtags_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was analyzed */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment of the tweet (positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_score" TEXT /* Sentiment score of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_type" TEXT /* Type of sentiment (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "trend" TEXT /* Trend identified from the tweets */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the trend */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the trend */,
  "trend_score" TEXT /* Trend score of the trend */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of trend (e.g., increasing, decreasing, stable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_articles" (
  "article_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each article */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the article */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the article */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the article was published */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the article */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Data source of the article */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("article_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "government_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the report was published */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the report */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Data source of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data accessed (tweets, news articles, government reports) */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
